page.content:
Table 1 presents the weight percentages of calcium (Ca), phosphorus (P), and the calcium to phosphorus ratio (Ca/P) for newly and cortical bone at 4 and 8 weeks. For the 4 weeks newly bone, Ca is 15.07 ± 2.83, P is 7.83 ± 1.56, and Ca/P is 1.93 ± 0.10. In the 4 weeks cortical bone, Ca is 19.77 ± 1.65, P is 10.27 ± 1.05, and Ca/P is 1.93 ± 0.07. For the 8 weeks newly bone, Ca is 17.33 ± 2.39, P is 8.90 ± 0.80, and Ca/P is 1.94 ± 0.10, while in the 8 weeks cortical bone, Ca is 21.33 ± 1.88, P is 11.03 ± 0.70, and Ca/P is 1.93 ± 0.05. 

Table 2 details the frequency percentages and lipid profiles across different genotypes.

page.content: For E2/E3, the frequency is 11%, total cholesterol (Total C) is 5.1 ± 1.04, LDL-C is 3.5 ± 0.87, HDL-C is 1.3 ± 0.25, and triacylglycerol is 1.5 ± 0.63. E2/E4 has a frequency of 2%, with total C at 5.3 ± 0.86, LDL-C at 3.5 ± 0.21, HDL-C at 1.5 ± 0.66, and triacylglycerol at 1.4 ± 0.43. E3/E3 shows a frequency of 55%, total C of 6.2 ± 0.86, LDL-C of 4.3 ± 0.75, HDL-C of 1.6 ± 0.31, and triacylglycerol of 1.2 ± 0.43. E3/E4, with a frequency of 28%, has total C at 6.2 ± 0.97, LDL-C at 4.3 ± 0.75, HDL-C at 1.6 ± 0.41, and triacylglycerol at 1.4 ± 0.64. Lastly, E4/E4 has a frequency of 4%, total C of 7.0 ± 0.91, LDL-C of 5.0 ± 0.87, HDL-C of 1.7 ± 0.22, and triacylglycerol of 1.3 ± 0.65.
